schema.appName: Mutual Fund Returns Calculator — Absolute & CAGR
schema.appDescription: Calculate absolute returns, CAGR, and money multiplier for any mutual fund. Enter NAV at purchase and current NAV to see your fund performance.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the inputs for the Mutual Fund Returns Calculator.

faq.q: What is the difference between absolute return and CAGR?
faq.a: Absolute return: total % gain from start to finish (e.g., 85% if NAV went from 100 to 185). CAGR: annualised return that accounts for time — 85% over 5 years = 13.1% CAGR. CAGR is the correct metric to compare funds over different periods.

faq.q: How do I find the NAV of a mutual fund?
faq.a: NAV is published daily on: AMFI website (amfiindia.com), fund house website, MF utility, and apps like Groww, Zerodha Coin, PayTM Money. Historical NAVs are available for any date. The NAV is the per-unit value of the fund's portfolio.

faq.q: What is a good CAGR for equity mutual funds in India?
faq.a: Index funds (Nifty 50): 12–15% CAGR over 10+ years. Actively managed large-cap: 12–16%. Mid-cap: 14–18%. Small-cap: 15–20% (with higher volatility). Debt funds: 6–8%. Always compare against the benchmark index over the same period — if the fund doesn't beat its benchmark, switch to an index fund.

faq.q: Is the Mutual Fund Returns Calculator free?

faq.a: No. Calculations run in your browser.

examples.heading: NAV 100 to 185 in 5 years
examples.text: Absolute return: 85%. CAGR: (185/100)^(1/5) - 1 = 13.1% p.a. Money multiplier: 1.85×. This outperforms the Nifty 50 historical average of 12% — good performance.

examples.heading: NAV 150 to 180 in 2 years
examples.text: Absolute return: 20%. CAGR: (180/150)^(1/2) - 1 = 9.5% p.a. Money multiplier: 1.2×. Underperforms Nifty 50. Consider switching to an index fund if consistent underperformance.
